name: One Dish Pork Chop Dinner
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Carrots, peeled and cut into 3-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Coat pork chops in flour; reserve excess flour.
2. In a large Dutch oven, melt butter over medium-high heat.
3. Brown pork chops on both sides.
4. Season with pepper and salt if desired.
5. Remove pork chops and set aside.
6. Stir reserved flour into the pan; cook until a paste forms.
7. Gradually whisk in 1 cup of apple juice; blend until smooth.
8. Return pork chops to the Dutch oven; cover and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.
9. Add potatoes, onions, carrots, and remaining apple juice.
10. Cover and bake an additional 30 minutes longer.
11. Top with shredded cabbage; cover and bake another 1-1/2 hours or until pork chops are tender, basting occasionally with juices.
Diabetic Exchanges: One serving prepared with margarine and without additional salt equals:
2 meat, 2 starch, 2 vegetable, 2 1/2 fat;
also 464 calories, 333mg sodium, 56 mg cholesterol, 43 gm carbohydrate, 19 gm protein, 24 gm fat.
SOURCE: Pat Waymire, Yellow Springs, OH, Country Woman Magazine May/June 93
POSTED BY: Jim Bodle 11/93
